creator: vsasjason@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 258603
patch against poudriere source (not ports tree)

Rationale (considering 3.4.2 release):

1. $CLEANJAIL being parsed in line 1208 using $OPTARG.
2. After that, default $CLEANJAIL value being incorrectly set in line 1273, referencing $CLEAN variable.
3. $CLEAN is being set to 0 in line 8861 of common.sh, which in being included in line 28 of jail.sh.
4. As as result, $CLEANJAIL _always_ receives '0' as value regardles off $OPTARG.
5. 'case' statement in line 124 doesn't match any of the variants and cleaning doesn't start.
Above is being fixed by changing line 1273.

Next issue with variable name: delete_jail declares local variable $clean_dir, however 'case' statement references $cleandir. Renamed.

Also, increased find depth for logs -- with '1' it doesn't work at all due to:
$ ll /usr/local/poudriere/data/logs/
total 9
drwxr-xr-x  8 root wheel 9 Mar  9 23:05 bulk

We need at least depth 2:
$ ll /usr/local/poudriere/data/logs/bulk/
total 72
-rw-r--r--    1 root wheel 1802 Mar  9 23:05 .data.json
drwxr-xr-x    3 root wheel    7 Mar  9 19:19 .html
drwxr-xr-x  726 root wheel  726 Mar  9 21:59 latest-per-pkg
drwxr-xr-x   34 root wheel   37 Dec 10 17:17 releng133-default
drwxr-xr-x    8 root wheel   11 Jan 20 20:00 releng134-default
drwxr-xr-x   32 root wheel   35 Jan 21 01:34 releng141-default
drwxr-xr-x   11 root wheel   14 Mar  9 23:05 releng142-default

But in fact, we need depth 5 because of logs are stored not only in '${POUDRIERE_DATA}/logs/bulk/<jail>', but in '${POUDRIERE_DATA}/logs/bulk/latest-per-pkg/<pkgname>/<pkgversion>/<jail>.log'
